brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 12.9% (D) with a valid contingency table derivation showing 16/124; Goes two levels deep — names the base rate fallacy and explains the directional inversion (P(A|B) vs P(B|A)) plus why tech co-founders don't discriminate strongly; Injects explicit [REASONING CONTEXT] header and names the specific fallacy, showing meta-awareness of the reasoning trap; Builds a complete 2×2 table and performs a sanity check comparing analyst's 80% to the computed ~13%, confirming directional coherence; Distinguishes the analyst's assumption from actual data and notes the practical magnitude difference ('meaningful but far from 80%'), though no explicit confidence intervals or sensitivity analysis; Presents only the contingency-table path without evaluating the Bayesian likelihood-ratio approach or confirming both methods converge; Every arithmetic step (16, 108, 124 total) is shown in the table and the final formula is explicitly stated, fully reproducible; Directly rejects the analyst's 80% figure and substitutes the correct posterior, explicitly labeling it as ignoring base rates — the precise negative gate pattern; Uses population-level frequency data (60% non-unicorn rate) explicitly, never lets the 80% estimate persist — both suppressed failure modes are visibly blocked; Bypasses the prescribed PRIOR→LR→Bayes sequential topology entirely, using a direct contingency-table approach with no branching gates, loops, or mid-process checks as specified
